understanding.function: Nuclear waste reprocessing system for extracting fissile material (uranium and plutonium) from spent nuclear fuel, capable of processing multiple fuel types (UOX, MOX, ERU/REU) with 1800 tHM/year capacity
understanding.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing and fissile material recovery
constraints: Product purity standards for downstream fuel fabrication
uncertainties: Specific technical processes for fissile material extraction not detailed
Exact safety systems and containment technologies unclear
Interface specifications with upstream/downstream systems undefined
Detailed regulatory requirements and compliance criteria not specified
Emergency response and degraded operation procedures partially defined
Waste management for non-fissile byproducts not addressed

candidates.name: PUREX Process
candidates.description: Plutonium Uranium Redox EXtraction process using solvent extraction with tributyl phosphate (TBP) for separating uranium and plutonium from spent nuclear fuel
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel reprocessing - aqueous solvent extraction
candidates.function_alignment: Direct match for uranium/plutonium extraction from spent fuel with established industrial scale capability
candidates.notes: Most widely used commercial reprocessing technology, proven at industrial scale

candidates.description: Alternative flowsheet using small channels and microfluidic technology for spent nuclear fuel reprocessing

candidates.notes: Emerging technology that may offer advantages in containment and process control

candidates.description: Wet technology reprocessing using organic reductants for Pu and Np ion reduction in spent nuclear fuel processing
candidates.function_alignment: Alternative chemical approach for actinide separation that could complement or replace traditional PUREX
candidates.notes: Focus on improved chemical control of actinide oxidation states

candidates.name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Established aqueous reprocessing technology using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from spent nuclear fuel through solvent extraction. Widely deployed globally with proven capability for UOX and MOX fuel processing.
candidates.function_alignment: Direct match - core technology for fissile material extraction from spent fuel with multi-fuel capability
candidates.notes: Industry standard process, mature technology with established safety protocols and regulatory framework

candidates.name: Advanced PUREX with Co-extraction (COEX)
candidates.description: Modified PUREX process that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together rather than separately, reducing proliferation risks while maintaining reprocessing efficiency. Designed for enhanced safeguards compliance.
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - advanced aqueous reprocessing
candidates.function_alignment: High alignment - addresses fissile material recovery with enhanced security features
candidates.notes: Proliferation-resistant variant suitable for commercial-scale operations

candidates.name: Pyroprocessing (Electrochemical Reprocessing)
candidates.description: High-temperature electrochemical process using molten salt electrolytes to separate actinides from fission products. Operates at 500°C with inherent proliferation resistance due to inability to separate pure plutonium.
candidates.function_alignment: Moderate alignment - alternative technology for fissile material recovery with different technical approach
candidates.notes: Emerging technology with potential advantages for certain fuel types, particularly metal fuels

candidates.name: UREX+ Process Suite
candidates.description: Modular aqueous separation process that extracts uranium first, followed by selective separation of transuranics and fission products. Designed to minimize waste streams and enhance proliferation resistance.
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - advanced separation processes
candidates.function_alignment: High alignment - specifically designed for efficient fissile material recovery with waste minimization
candidates.notes: Modular design allows customization for specific fuel types and processing requirements

candidates.name: Remote Handling and Hot Cell Systems
candidates.description: Automated robotic systems and shielded hot cell facilities for handling highly radioactive spent fuel during reprocessing operations. Essential infrastructure for maintaining ALARA principles in high-radiation environments.
candidates.function_alignment: Critical supporting technology - enables safe operation of reprocessing systems
candidates.notes: Essential for meeting radiation protection requirements and operational availability targets

candidates.name: Continuous Monitoring and Safeguards Systems
candidates.description: Real-time nuclear material accountancy and control systems including neutron detection, gamma spectroscopy, and process monitoring to ensure material balance and regulatory compliance during reprocessing.
candidates.function_alignment: Essential supporting system - required for regulatory compliance and material control
candidates.notes: Critical for meeting international safeguards obligations and domestic regulatory requirements

candidates.name: Vitrification Systems for High-Level Waste
candidates.description: Glass matrix immobilization technology for high-level radioactive waste streams from reprocessing operations. Converts liquid waste into stable glass forms for long-term storage and disposal.
candidates.function_alignment: Supporting technology - manages waste streams from fissile material extraction processes
candidates.notes: Required for complete fuel cycle closure and waste management compliance

candidates.name: Modular Construction and Standardized Components
candidates.description: Prefabricated, standardized process modules designed for nuclear reprocessing facilities to reduce construction time, improve quality control, and enhance maintainability while meeting nuclear safety standards.
candidates.function_alignment: Supporting approach - enables efficient deployment of reprocessing capacity
candidates.notes: Can accelerate facility deployment while maintaining quality and safety standards

ranked.name: PUREX Process
ranked.description: Plutonium Uranium Redox EXtraction process using solvent extraction with tributyl phosphate (TBP) for separating uranium and plutonium from spent nuclear fuel
ranked.domain: Nuclear fuel reprocessing - aqueous solvent extraction
ranked.gap_analysis: Direct match for required function with proven industrial-scale capability for 1800 tHM/year capacity and multi-fuel processing.
ranked.notes: Industry standard technology with decades of operational experience at facilities like La Hague

ranked.name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
ranked.description: Established aqueous reprocessing technology using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from spent nuclear fuel through solvent extraction. Widely deployed globally with proven capability for UOX and MOX fuel processing.
ranked.gap_analysis: Perfect alignment with requirements - mature technology capable of processing all specified fuel types at required capacity.
ranked.notes: Duplicate entry of PUREX process with additional detail on multi-fuel capability

ranked.name: Spent Fuel Dissolution and Reprocessing Systems
ranked.description: Comprehensive spent fuel dissolution and reprocessing processes covering multiple fuel types and extraction methods
ranked.domain: Nuclear fuel reprocessing - dissolution and extraction
ranked.gap_analysis: Complete system approach covering all process steps from dissolution to fissile material recovery for multiple fuel types.
ranked.notes: Integrated approach suitable for UOX, MOX, and ERU fuel processing requirements

ranked.name: Solvent Extraction for Nuclear Power
ranked.description: Industrial-scale solvent extraction systems specifically designed for nuclear power fuel cycle applications
ranked.domain: Nuclear fuel reprocessing - industrial solvent extraction
ranked.gap_analysis: Industrial-scale technology directly applicable to 1800 tHM/year capacity requirements with proven scaling methods.
ranked.notes: Proven industrial technology with established capacity scaling methods

ranked.name: Advanced PUREX with Co-extraction (COEX)
ranked.description: Modified PUREX process that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together rather than separately, reducing proliferation risks while maintaining reprocessing efficiency. Designed for enhanced safeguards compliance.
ranked.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - advanced aqueous reprocessing
ranked.gap_analysis: Addresses fissile material recovery with enhanced security features, suitable for French regulatory environment.
ranked.notes: Proliferation-resistant variant suitable for commercial-scale operations

ranked.name: UREX+ Process Suite
ranked.description: Modular aqueous separation process that extracts uranium first, followed by selective separation of transuranics and fission products. Designed to minimize waste streams and enhance proliferation resistance.
ranked.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - advanced separation processes
ranked.gap_analysis: Specifically designed for efficient fissile material recovery but requires adaptation for 1800 tHM/year industrial scale.
ranked.notes: Modular design allows customization for specific fuel types and processing requirements

ranked.name: Hydro-Process Recycling Systems
ranked.description: Chemical basis for separating recycling materials using aqueous hydro-processes for nuclear fuel cycle applications
ranked.gap_analysis: Aqueous-based separation technology suitable for large-scale fissile material recovery but lacks specific industrial implementation details.
ranked.notes: Comprehensive approach to material separation in nuclear fuel recycling

ranked.name: Actinide Extraction Chemistry Systems
ranked.description: Advanced extraction chemistry methods specifically designed for actinide ion separation and recovery
ranked.gap_analysis: Specialized chemistry for fissile material extraction but requires integration into complete industrial-scale system.
ranked.notes: Fundamental chemistry advances that enable improved separation processes

ranked.name: Organic Reductant-Based Reprocessing
ranked.description: Wet technology reprocessing using organic reductants for Pu and Np ion reduction in spent nuclear fuel processing
ranked.gap_analysis: Alternative chemical approach for actinide separation but lacks proven industrial-scale implementation for required capacity.
ranked.notes: Focus on improved chemical control of actinide oxidation states

ranked.name: Pyroprocessing (Electrochemical Reprocessing)
ranked.description: High-temperature electrochemical process using molten salt electrolytes to separate actinides from fission products. Operates at 500°C with inherent proliferation resistance due to inability to separate pure plutonium.
ranked.gap_analysis: Alternative technology for fissile material recovery but not proven at industrial scale for oxide fuel processing.
ranked.notes: Emerging technology with potential advantages for certain fuel types, particularly metal fuels

ranked.name: Process Intensification Reprocessing
ranked.description: Alternative flowsheet using small channels and microfluidic technology for spent nuclear fuel reprocessing
ranked.gap_analysis: Emerging technology with potential safety advantages but significant scaling challenges to reach 1800 tHM/year capacity.
ranked.notes: Emerging technology that may offer advantages in containment and process control
